title: Maintenance Administrator
description: Unilin Insulation Ireland is currently recruiting for a Maintenance Administrator. As a Maintenance Administrator, you will play a key role in supporting the Maintenance Department through efficient administration, data management, and coordination. This role is integral to ensuring the smooth operation of maintenance activities, enhancing equipment reliability, and contributing to overall operational excellence across the organisation. \#insulationUK **.** **Job Title: Maintenance Administrator** **Reporting to:** Maintenance Manager **Responsibilities:** Provide maintenance administration and proactive support to the Maintenance Department. **Key Attributes:** * Excellent numerical and analytical skills * Ability to meet deadlines with good problem\-solving skills * Excellent communication skills * Highly motivated with proven ability to work on own initiative * Ability to multitask with strong attention to detail * Team player who will work with others to achieve company goals * Methodical with excellent organisational skills * Good PC skills with knowledge of data analysis \& presentation * Experience specifying \& categorising engineering equipment details an advantage * Proficient in Microsoft Word \& Excel * Business Administration / Management qualification desirable * SAP Experience is highly desirable **Key Tasks and Duties:** * Administration of SAP Plant Maintenance (PM) and Material Management (MM) data * Management of goods in / good out for the maintenance department. * Carry out regular stock counts, ensuring minimum stock levels are maintained and address deviations. * Maintenance stores to be 6S organized, and stock / critical spares levels maintained. * Actively sourcing reduced cost solutions with existing and new suppliers. * Working with the purchasing department to effectively source parts and arrange delivery/collections. * Assign new stock items to their proper locations and assets within the SAP system. * Providing daily, weekly reports on assets, costs, KPI’s etc. * Arranging contractor site visits and liaise with the Maintenance Team to ensure the work is complete. * Administration of all statutory checks, contractors, scheduling, service reports, and associated H\&S. * Administration and control of all maintenance documentation, drawings and training packs. * Actively participating in Continuous Improvement projects and site projects. * Proactively providing solutions and making suggestions improving site and maintenance operations. * Issuing out relevant training packs and information to Maintenance Technicians. * Ensuring all policies and procedures are strictly adhered to. * Responsible for ensuring a safe, clean working environment. * Regularly providing progress reports to the engineering management team. * Responsible as part of a bigger team for ensuring that all operational targets are achieved. * Undertake any other tasks \& training appropriate to the position, as requested by management * Work within the ethos and standards of the company. ### **What can you expect?** Unilin is a fast\-growing, profitable and bustling group that is active internationally. Innovation is our driving force. Investing in people, technology and sustainable entrepreneurship is characteristic of our culture. Unilin thrives thanks to a team of colleagues that pride themselves on professionalism and entrepreneurship. In exchange for your expertise, creativity and dynamism, Unilin offers an attractive salary supplemented with fringe benefits, career opportunities, challenges abroad and a wide range of training options. ### **Who are we?** Unilin is a worldwide leader in interior design and construction materials. Our floors, (decorative) panels, insulation solutions and technologies can be found in the homes and workplaces of millions of people and public spaces around the world. Our team of 8600 passionate employees across 105 locations in multiple countries aims to improve people's quality of life by launching new innovations and constantly pushing boundaries. Our commitment to excellence and extensive know\-how enable us to make beautiful, sustainable products of the highest quality. And our efforts are rewarded: together we realised a €3\.1 billion turnover in 2022\. We are also proud to be a part of Mohawk Industries, the global market leader in flooring with a $11\.6 billion turnover and 41,000 employees.

title: Manufacturing Technician
description: **Job Details:** ================ **Job Description:** -------------------- Intel Ireland is a leading centre for semiconductor manufacturing and technology development, producing advanced process technologies that enable Intel products used around the world. Our teams work in a highly automated, high\-volume manufacturing environment, focused on safety, innovation, operational excellence, and continuous improvement. This is a shift\-based role. Candidates must be able to work on a monthly rotating shift pattern. Exact shift details will be shared during the interview stage. **Responsibilities/What you'll do:** As an **Experienced Manufacturing Technician** at Intel Ireland, you will support wafer manufacturing operations in a high\-volume semiconductor environment. You will bring strong technical capability in equipment maintenance, troubleshooting, and operational support, while helping to maintain safe, efficient, and high\-quality manufacturing performance. In this role, you will: * Perform functions associated with all aspects of wafer production, including manufacturing operations, equipment handling, and process\-related activities. * Support equipment operation within the manufacturing area to meet safety, quality, output, and cost targets. * Conduct preventative and corrective maintenance on manufacturing equipment in a regulated environment. * Troubleshoot equipment and process issues, complete repairs, and support defect reduction activities. * Use technical manuals, specifications, drawings, diagrams, schematics, and verbal instructions to perform assigned tasks. * Apply structured problem\-solving techniques and use appropriate tools and test equipment to address production and equipment challenges. * Support installation, repair, operation, and performance improvement of manufacturing equipment. * Work collaboratively with engineering, manufacturing, and operations teams to resolve technical issues and improve equipment reliability. * Help drive operational and maintenance improvements within your assigned area. * Contribute to a team culture focused on inclusion, diversity, accountability, and continuous improvement. * Support business needs that may require travel on assignment for varying durations. **Qualifications:** ------------------- **Minimum Qualifications/Experience:** * Candidates must have a relevant technical qualification (e.g. B.Sc., B.Eng.) in an approved Science or Engineering discipline which would include but not exclusively * A National Framework of Qualifications (NFQ) Level 6 or Level 7 qualification in a technical discipline, or a recognised trade qualification in Electrical, Plumbing, or Instrumentation. * 4\+ years of experience in technical, semiconductor, pharmaceutical, biomedical, or medical device manufacturing. * 4\+ years of hands\-on preventative and corrective maintenance experience in a regulated environment. * 4\+ years of experience in electronics, equipment troubleshooting, installation, repair, and operation in a regulated environment. In addition, viable candidates must meet the following requirements: * Strong analytical and problem\-solving skills. * The ability to work effectively in a diverse team environment and manage multiple priorities. * The ability to work with minimal supervision and adapt to changing business needs. * A proactive approach to driving operational and maintenance improvements. * Commitment to promoting inclusion, diversity, and positive cultural change within the team. * The ability to work on a monthly rotating shift pattern. **Preferred Experience:** The following would be an advantage: * Hands\-on maintenance experience with semiconductor equipment. * Experience influencing the creation of new specifications, procedures, and business processes related to equipment maintenance. * Knowledge of process control systems. * Experience troubleshooting using schematics and complex diagrams. * Experience establishing and supporting a work environment focused on continuous improvement. * Knowledge of lean manufacturing disciplines, including high precision maintenance and smart place work organization. * Experience maintaining vacuum systems, precision mechanical equipment, or RF systems **Job Type:** ------------- Experienced Hire **Shift:** ---------- Shift 1 A (Ireland) **Primary Location:** --------------------- Ireland, Leixlip **Additional Locations:** ------------------------- **Business group:** ------------------- Intel Foundry strives to make every facet of semiconductor manufacturing state\-of\-the\-art while delighting our customers \- from delivering cutting\-edge silicon process and packaging technology leadership for the AI era, enabling our customers to design leadership products, global manufacturing scale and supply chain, through the continuous yield improvements to advanced packaging all the way to final test and assembly. We ensure our foundry customers' products receive our utmost focus in terms of service, technology enablement and capacity commitments. Employees in the Foundry Technology Manufacturing are part of a worldwide factory network that designs, develops, manufactures, and assembly/test packages the compute devices to improve the lives of every person on Earth. **Posting Statement:** ---------------------- All qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to race, color, religion, religious creed, sex, national origin, ancestry, age, physical or mental disability, medical condition, genetic information, military and veteran status, marital status, pregnancy, gender, gender expression, gender identity, sexual orientation, or any other characteristic protected by local law, regulation, or ordinance. **Position of Trust** --------------------- N/A **EU Salary** ------------- We offer a total compensation package that ranks among the best in the industry. Annual Salary Range for this job which could be performed in Ireland is: €39,800\.00 \- €62,200\.00\&\#xa;\&\#xa;The compensation range shown in this job posting represents the minimum and maximum target compensation for this position. Within this range, individual pay is determined by job related skills, experience, relevant education or training. As part of our total compensation approach, the final offer may include additional pay components, shift differentials and benefits. Your recruiting team will share the confirmed base salary along with any applicable total compensation elements upon employment offer delivery. **Work Model for this Role** This role will require an on\-site presence. \* Job posting details (such as work model, location or time type) are subject to change. *

title: Material Handler - based in Ireland
description: Introduction to the job Within ASML the Customer Support (CS) group is responsible for the maintenance, repair and continuous improvement of ASML systems at customer locations. CS EU EUV UIR team performs Upgrades, Installs and Relocations (UIR) in Europe for our EUV customers. This Material Handler role (min 2 FTE) will be part of the local engineering team for synergy. As a team with very experienced and highly motivated engineers, we are making a start in this new organization and aiming for success. Role and responsibilities CS EUV UIR Material Handler within ASML the CS EU UIR department is responsible for the installation of EUV products at customer locations in Europe. The Material Handler ensures material and tool readiness \+ delivery to the UIR Engineers in the System Working Area (SWA). Your role is to support the installation and upgrade of leading\-edge lithographic systems, at the customer site, within specification, on time, with quality exceeding the customer’s expectations. Deliverables: * Physically move materials, using the appropriate/described transportation method(s), within the clean room and support the movement of material from Laydown Area (LDA) to the SWA. * Maintain thorough material administration of move in/ out parts, making sure material is where it needs to be at the required time (manage and track movement of material). * Maintain a clean and organized work area with an optimized workflow and overview to support daily UIR operation * Training \& coaching fellow MRs * Support UIR Disturbance Review Board (DRB). * Identify, initiate and drive improvements Responsibilities: * Ensure the required tools and materials are available at the right time, based on the daily plan and actual status of the installation, at the machine (SWA). * Return materials, tools and packaging material to ASML or to the customer warehouse. * Manage the storage of materials, ensuring material won’t get lost and by keeping the work are clean \+ organized. * Check tools and materials for deviations in their SERV/12NC number, quality and cleanliness. Take action to get deviations resolved. * Solve wrong deliveries and wrong material delivery issues. * Reporting defects, missing or wrong parts and tools, take corrective and preventive action. * Work with the EF/ CS install project team to ensure tooling preventive maintenance and calibrations are up to date and scheduled with regards to installation planning. * Administrative processing of transactions such as maintaining the build\-in/ build\-out list in order to maintain a correct configuration of the installed machine in SAP . * Ordering of material and assisting the delivery of spare parts and special tooling during escalations * Work with Install Team and local Logistics team to process material notifications for DOA’s * Identify, initiate and drive improvements to reduce Install Costs \& Cycle Time and/or improve Safety \& Quality. * Highly proficient in SAP, MS Excel, Office and various database software programs. Education and experience * Bachelor degree is preferred but not mandatory. * Experience in semiconductor field or logistics technician is preferred but not mandatory. * Knowledge of logistic process, service order process is mandatory. * Knowledge of SAP and Plan on is a plus. **Skills** Working at the cutting edge of tech, you’ll always have new challenges and new problems to solve – and working together is the only way to do that. You won’t work in a silo. Instead, you’ll be part of a creative, dynamic work environment where you’ll collaborate with supportive colleagues. There is always space for creative and unique points of view. You’ll have the flexibility and trust to choose how best to tackle tasks and solve problems. To thrive in this job, you’ll need the following skills: * Handle Microsoft office tools skillfully (especially for excel) * Able to work in a very dynamic and fast paced environment. * Open for cultural differences and able to work with site of different culture and way of working. * Motivated, pro\-active, self\-driven and flexible. * Good verbal and written in English Other information Travel is estimated up to 60%. With the organization in transition, this percentage is an estimation and can be revisited in mutual agreement. *This position requires access to controlled technology, as defined in the* *United States* Export Administration Regulations (15 C.F.R. * 730, et seq.). Qualified candidates must be legally authorized to access such controlled technology prior to beginning work. Business demands may require ASML to proceed with candidates who are immediately eligible to access controlled technology. **Inclusion and diversity** ASML is an Equal Opportunity Employer that values and respects the importance of a diverse and inclusive workforce. It is the policy of the company to recruit, hire, train and promote persons in all job titles without regard to race, color, religion, sex, age, national origin, veteran status, disability, sexual orientation, or gender identity. We recognize that inclusion and diversity is a driving force in the success of our company. Need to know more about applying for a job at ASML? Read our frequently asked questions.

title: Mechanical Shift Technician
description: **Background:** Veolia is Ireland’s leading environmental services company. We provide a comprehensive range of energy, waste and water solutions and are dedicated to carbon reduction, protecting the environment and building the circular economy. We offer all employees a benefits package as standard which includes full VHI cover, pension and education assistance. When you see the world as we do, you see the chance to help the world take better care of its resources, and help it become a better place for everyone. It's why we're looking for someone who's just as committed as we are, to push for genuine change and bring our ambition of Ecological Transformation to life. We know that everyone here at Veolia can help us work alongside our communities, look after the environment, and contribute to our inclusive culture. **Mechanical Shift Technician** **Location: Blanchardstown** **Duration: Permanent** **Hours \- Shift** **(2 days, 2 nights, 4 days off)** **Overview of the role;** The Utilities Technician is responsible for the safe, compliant, and reliable operation and maintenance of clean utilities, black utilities, HVAC and refrigeration, fire alarm/suppression systems, wastewater treatment, and medium\-voltage (MV) electrical distribution that support GMP manufacturing and life safety. The role operates within controlled cleanroom and technical environments and requires adherence to cGMP, GDP, and EHS standards, delivering high availability of critical services on a rotating shift. **Day to Day of the role;** * Clean Utilities Operations and Maintenance: Operate, monitor, and maintain purified water (PW), water for injection (WFI), clean steam, clean compressed air, and clean gases. Execute sanitisation/heat cycles, support sampling, trend CQAs/CPPs, manage biofilm risk, and ensure system state of control for GMP use points. * Black Utilities Operations and Maintenance: Maintain boilers/steam distribution, condensate return, chillers, cooling towers, plant compressed air/vacuum, and heating hot water. Optimise energy efficiency and reliability, ensuring compliance with statutory requirements (e.g., legionella control, pressure systems). * HVAC \& Refrigeration: Operate and maintain AHUs serving cleanrooms and labs to meet temperature, RH, and differential pressure specifications. Perform filter changes, support HEPA integrity testing, and troubleshoot airflows. Maintain refrigeration assets (DX systems, packaged chillers, cold rooms/freezers), ensuring F\-gas compliance, leak checks, and performance optimisation. * Fire Alarm \& Suppression Systems: Support inspection, testing, and maintenance of fire detection (addressable, aspirating) and suppression interfaces (sprinkler, gas where applicable). Execute approved isolations, issue/track red tags, and complete functional restoration tests in line with EHS and insurance requirements. * WWTP/Effluent: Operate and maintain screens, equalisation, biological/chemical treatment, DAF/MBBR/IFAS (as applicable), clarifiers, sludge handling, and dosing systems. Monitor and trend key parameters (pH, DO, ORP, temperature, conductivity, flow), coordinate sampling, and ensure compliance with discharge licences and incident response procedures. * MV Electrical Support: Support safe operation of MV switchgear and transformers. Assist authorised persons with switching plans, LOTO, and permits; monitor power quality and resilience, including coordination with UPS and generators. Engage with ESB and vendors as directed by site electrical authority. * Control Systems \& Alarm Response: Monitor systems via BMS/SCADA (e.g., NQBMS/QBMS/MCS) and fire panels. Respond to alarms per PRCs/SOPs, perform initial troubleshooting, and promptly escalate GMP/critical events to protect product and operations. Document actions and shift handovers. * Preventive \& Reactive Maintenance: Execute PMs, CMs, and predictive tasks; coordinate vendor interventions; manage spare parts; and close work orders in the CMMS to GDP standards with accurate parts/time entries and attachments. * Permitting, Isolations \& Safety: Prepare RAMS and permits to work; perform mechanical/electrical isolations (LOTO) and fire system isolations; verify and redline P\&IDs/SLDs/C\&E as needed; conduct pre\- and post\-work checks and return\-to\-service verifications. * Compliance \& Documentation: Work to cGMP/GDP, Irish/EU statutory sop s sisa tem; prepare logs, deviations, change controls, and CAPAs; support internal/external audits and regulatory inspections. * Troubleshooting \& Root Cause: Apply structured fault\-finding across mechanical, electrical, and control domains; support RCAs and implement corrective and preventive actions that sustainably address failure modes. * Commissioning \& Projects: Support FAT/SAT, commissioning, and handover for utilities and vendor packaged equipment; review test protocols, verify I/O, and ensure asset data and documentation are complete. * Continuous Improvement: Identify and implement reliability and energy/water/steam efficiency improvements; contribute to SOP updates, standard work, and best practices; track and report KPIs (uptime, alarm recurrence, energy metrics).Working in Controlled/Clean Environments * Maintain cleanroom discipline, gowning, and behaviour appropriate to ISO 8–ISO 5 areas as required by task. * Ensure HVAC parameters, pressure cascades, and filtration states are preserved during maintenance; plan interventions to minimise contamination risk. * Apply contamination control strategies (tool control, point\-of\-use sanitisation, clean breaks, material/personnel flows) and follow microbiological/particulate control procedures. * Observe GDP for all entries (indelible, timely, attributable, legible), and ensure use\-point impact assessments are completed before/after interventions. **Requirements for the role;** * Minimum Education/Trade: Recognised Irish trade qualification (QQI Level 6 Craft) in Electrical, Mechanical, Refrigeration, or Instrumentation, or Relevant third\-level qualification (Level 7 or Level 8\) in Mechanical, Electrical, Building Services, Mechatronics, or related engineering discipline. * 2\+ years in pharma/biopharma or regulated manufacturing preferred, with hands\-on exposure to clean utilities and at least two of: black utilities, HVAC/refrigeration, fire systems, WWTP, MV. * Operation and maintenance of PW/WFI/clean steam and cleanroom HVAC. * BMS/SCADA operation, alarm management, trend analysis, and setpoint governance. * Mechanical/electrical troubleshooting; reading P\&IDs, SLDs, cause\-and\-effect, fire strategies. * LOTO, permits to work, RAMS; basic water/steam chemistry; balancing/commissioning (TAB) support. * CMMS proficiency; vendor coordination and documentation control. * Advantageous: Steam systems optimisation (BOAS/steam awareness), WWTP operator training, ATEX awareness, power quality basics, refrigeration diagnostics. * Safe Pass, Manual Handling, Confined Space, Working at Height/MEWP. * F\-gas Category I/II (for refrigeration work). * First Aid Responder/Fire Marshal. * Electrical authorisation up to MV (as applicable to site). * Safety \& Compliance Focus: Makes safe decisions, follows EHS and life\-safety procedures rigorously. * Problem Solving \& Ownership: Structured diagnostics, prioritises critical alarms, and sees issues through to closure. * Communication \& Documentation: Clear handovers, accurate GDP records, and effective stakeholder updates. * Collaboration: Works cross\-functionally with Manufacturing, QA, EHS, Facilities, and vendors. * Continuous Improvement Mindset: Data\-driven, proactive in reliability and energy performance. * Work across plant rooms, cleanrooms, rooftops, WWTP, and outdoors; use of PPE; occasional lifting; work at height or in confined spaces subject to training and permits. * Participation in site emergency response as assigned. **Please note:** If you are an EU/EEA national, you will be asked to show proof of right to work in Ireland. If you are a non\-EU/EEA national , you will require current and valid permission to work and reside in the Republic of Ireland. Information on legislation and guides to the procedures in relation to obtaining greencard permits ,work permits, spousal/dependant permits is available on the Department of Jobs, Enterprise and Innovation website.

title: Met us at NIBRT? Start your career in Medical Information with ProPharma
description: For the past 20 years, ProPharma has improved the health and wellness of patients by providing advice and expertise that empowers biotech, med device, and pharmaceutical organizations of all sizes to confidently advance scientific breakthroughs and introduce new therapies. ProPharma partners with its clients through an advise\-build\-operate model across the complete product lifecycle. With deep domain expertise in regulatory sciences, clinical research solutions, quality \& compliance, pharmacovigilance, medical information, and R\&D technology, ProPharma offers an end\-to\-end suite of fully customizable consulting solutions that de\-risk and accelerate our partners’ most high\-profile drug and device programs. Did we meet you at the National Institute for Bioprocessing Research and Training careers event? Are you a recent (or soon\-to\-be) graduate in Life Sciences, Nursing, Pharmacy, or Medicine with a strong interest in patient safety and the pharmaceutical industry? If you're curious about where your degree could take you next, we’d love to stay connected. **Why consider a career in Medical Information?** Medical Information (MI) is a unique entry point into the pharmaceutical and biotech industry. It sits at the heart of patient safety, scientific communication, and real\-world healthcare impact. **At ProPharma, Our Medical Information Teams** * Provide accurate, evidence\-based information on medicines and devices * Support healthcare professionals, patients, and the public * Play a key role in drug safety and regulatory compliance * Work in a fast\-paced, collaborative, and global environment It’s a role where you can apply your scientific knowledge from day one—while continuing to learn and grow. What could your future role look like? **As a Multilingual Medical Information Specialist (MLMIS), You Would** * Respond to medical and safety enquiries via phone, email, and digital platforms * Communicate complex scientific information in a clear, professional way * Support pharmacovigilance activities (e.g. adverse event reporting) * Work with international teams and global pharmaceutical clients * Develop expertise in therapeutic areas and medical communication We regularly look for graduates with fluency in English plus another European language (e.g. French, German, Spanish, Italian, Dutch). **What We Look For In Graduates** * A degree in a Life Science or healthcare\-related discipline * Strong communication skills and attention to detail * Interest in patient safety, medical communication, or the pharma industry * A proactive, curious mindset and willingness to learn * Language skills (depending on role requirements) **Why join ProPharma?** * Structured graduate\-friendly onboarding with full training provided * Clear career progression – many of our leaders started in entry\-level MI roles * Flexible, remote working opportunities * Exposure to global pharmaceutical clients and projects * A supportive, collaborative, and multicultural environment **Stay connected with us** Even if you're not ready to apply just yet, we’d love to hear from you. Register your interest to be considered for upcoming Multilingual Medical Information Specialist (MLMIS) opportunities. Simply submit your CV and we’ll keep you in mind for future roles that match your profile. We celebrate our differences and strive to create a workplace where each person can be their authentic self. We are committed to diversity, equity, and inclusion. Employees are encouraged to unleash their innovative, collaborative, and entrepreneurial spirits. With a holistic approach as an Equal Opportunity Employer, we provide a safe space where all employees feel empowered to succeed. All applications to roles at ProPharma are personally reviewed by a member of our recruitment team. We do not rely on AI screening tools to support our hiring process. You will always receive an outcome to your application so that you have an answer from us \- whether you're successful or not. Whilst ProPharma supports remote working, we also recognise the value that comes from in person collaboration. As such, we encourage any new hires that are based within a reasonably short commute of one of our offices to work on a hybrid basis and spend some time working from that office location, as agreed with your manager. All applications will be treated on their own merit and candidates will not be at any advantage or disadvantage based on their proximity to an office. \*\*\*ProPharma Group does not accept unsolicited resumes from recruiters/third parties. Please, no phone calls or emails to anyone regarding this posting.\*\*\*

title: Microsoft D365 Technical Consultant

Platform, and proactively share knowledge and insights with the team. * Offering recommendations on best practices and industry standards in relation to Microsoft Dynamics 365 and related technologies. **About you:** -------------- * Minimum of 5 years experience as a Microsoft Dynamics 365 Technical Consultant or a similar role, working with Microsoft Dynamics 365 or the Power internal teams. * Self\-motivated and able to work independently, while also being a strong team player. * Flexibility to work in a hybrid work environment, with the ability to be present in the office when required. **If you are a Microsoft Dynamics 365 Consultant looking for an exciting opportunity to work with a diverse range of clients and make a significant impact, we would love to hear from you.** **Why you will enjoy working at BearingPoint Ireland?** ------------------------------------------------------- We work in an open and collaborative way, where new ideas are always BearingPoint is an environment where potential and high performance is recognised and rewarded. **What we offer:** ------------------ You will receive a competitive salary where you are rewarded for both your individual performance and company performance. You will also receive a best\-in\-class benefits package including holidays, private health insurance, pension contributions, gym/club subscription, wellness programmes, mobile phone and much more. In addition, we offer: **Hybrid working –** where each team has autonomy to determine their in\-person and remote working schedule. **Continuous learning –** we understand that it is imperative to invest in our people's development and we provide external training at prestigious universities such as Oxford and Yale **Social events –** our people perform best when they have a good work life balance, monthly events for all to attend **Diversity** – we are committed to having a diverse workforce where each individual feels they belong and can thrive in a work environment with a strong sense of community **Mentoring \& coaching –** every employee has a development manager to help them grow their career with feedback via continuous conversations **Giving Back –** you will have an opportunity to join our many giving back initiatives including our commitment to sustainability. The company operates in four units: Consulting, Solutions, Business Services, and Ventures. Consulting covers the advisory business; Solutions provides the tools for successful digital transformation, advanced analytics and regulatory requirements; Business Services provides managed services beyond SaaS; Ventures drives the financing and development of start\-ups. title: Mobile Patrol Driver
description: ### **About The Role** **About The Role** **Job Title:** Mobile Patrol Driver **Location: Co Donegal** **Hourly Rate:** €18 **Hours:** 36–48 hours per week (12\-hour shifts) **Benefits:** * Company vehicle supplied * Overtime Pay and Sunday Rates of €3\.44 per hour * Night allowance of €20\.00 * 21 days annual leave * Employee discounts over a wide range of brands, service, hotels etc. * Progression opportunities **Role overview** Bidvest Noonan is currently recruiting for a team of **Mobile Patrol Drivers** to join our expanding security team. This is an exciting opportunity to work on a prestigious client site, ensuring a safe and secure environment through proactive patrolling and rapid incident response. The role reports directly to the **Operations Manager** and requires a professional individual with a commitment to high standards of service and security. ***We require a valid PSA licence and full clean driver’s licence.*** **Key Responsibilities of the Mobile Patrol Driver:** * Respond promptly to alarm activations and any breaches of security. * Perform scheduled and random mobile patrols across various sites. * Complete detailed, time\-stamped digital incident reports (including photos) using the company app. * Lock and unlock client premises according to schedule. * Liaise courteously with clients, service users, and property management. * Collaborate with the Bidvest Noonan National Call Centre for coordination. * Conduct regular vehicle checks via the company app. * Maintain a professional image and adhere to company uniform standards. * Identify, assess, and report potential risks or hazards. * Set a professional example when conducting visits to static security sites. **Key Requirements of the Mobile Patrol Driver:** * A **valid PSA licence** (essential) * A **full, clean driving licence** * Minimum of **3 years’ security industry experience** * Strong observational and reporting skills * Excellent punctuality, reliability, strong ability to work on initiative * Good working knowledge of the Dublin area * High attention to detail and ability to work under pressure * Strong written communication for accurate report writing ### **About Us** ‘For nearly 50 years, we’ve played a vital role in helping our customers succeed. Built on strong, long‑standing relationships, our business is powered by people who are deeply connected to the communities where we live and work. Our purpose guides every action and decision we make, “to proudly create safer and healthier communities today, for tomorrow.” It reflects who we are, what we stand for, and the impact we strive to make every day. Across our team of 27,000 dedicated professionals from 157 countries, that purpose comes to life through commitment, care, and a shared responsibility to the people we serve—now and for generations to come. **'When you join Bidvest Noonan, you’re joining a team where your work has real impact, where your career can grow, where you belong, and where you help shape a safer, healthier future for us all.’**

title: Paraplanner
description: 5 Jun 2026 Dublin, IE, IE **About Goodbody** Goodbody is a leading financial services firm, committed to serving and growing client relationships for 150 years. We are a full service, investment\-led business offering wealth management, asset management, investment banking and private equity services. Our staff of over 400 work in offices across Dublin, London, Cork and Galway. We are proud to offer prospective employees a professional, collegial work environment that encourages diversity of thought and background. Our reputation in the Irish market for putting customers first is as consistent as the company’s long\-standing commitment to giving back to the communities that we work in. **About the Role** As a core member of Goodbody Wealth Management’s Technical Support Services team, the Paraplanner partners with Financial Planning Specialists and Strategic Advisors to convert complex client information into clear, compliant and client‑centric financial plans for high‑ and ultra‑high‑net‑worth clients. You will build robust cash‑flow models (Voyant), prepare and iterate planning recommendations, coordinate case conferences, and manage documentation and fee tracking from engagement through post‑plan follow‑through. You will report to the Financial Planning Practice Manager and contribute to the ongoing development of Goodbody’s planning methodology and client experience. This is a permanent role with a hybrid working model. **Key Responsibilities** * Prepare comprehensive financial plans, including cash‑flow modelling (Voyant), net‑worth projections and scenario analysis. * Coordinate planning workflows: case conferences, agendas, draft iterations and stakeholder reviews to agreed timelines. * Maintain accurate records of meetings, data sources and assumptions; collate and interpret third‑party information. * Manage onboarding for planning engagements, ensuring adherence to protocols and the Consumer Protection Code (CPC). * Track plan delivery, fees and the execution of recommendations after plan sign‑off. * Contribute to continuous improvement of templates, process maps and training materials as regulations and best practice evolve. **Qualifications and Experience** ***Professional \& Academic Requirements*** The successful candidate will hold relevant third‑level and professional qualifications. The following are considered particularly suitable: * Business, financial or investment‑related third‑level qualification * Qualified Financial Advisor (QFA) * Certified Financial Planner (CFP) or a willingness to complete the in the near future ***Experience*** * At least three years' experience in a financial services client advisory environment. * Experience in the areas of pensions, retirement planning and estate planning beneficial. * Familiarity with the operational and systems requirements of CPC and other regulatory requirements. ***Specific systems knowledge*** * Strong digital literacy, MS Office including Excel, Word and Powerpoint, CRM systems, and preferably financial planning tools such as Voyant. * Strong knowledge of financial needs analysis software and market intelligence software essential. **Ideal Candidate Profile** We are seeking an ambitious, motivated professional who demonstrates: * Innovative and solution\-oriented, capable of challenging existing approaches and contributing new perspectives. * Proven ability to deliver tangible outcomes through personal initiative and technical excellence. * A strong team orientation, thriving in a dynamic environment with multiple stakeholders and evolving client needs. * Strong relationship‑building capability across multidisciplinary teams. * High levels of digital literacy, analytical ability and attention to detail. * Strong organizational, communication and presentation skills with a commitment to delivering client‑centric outcomes. * Commitment to the highest ethical and professional standards in client work and internal collaboration. If you meet most of the above criteria and are excited about the role, we encourage you to apply. **Diversity \& Inclusion** * Goodbody is committed to achieving gender balance in leadership and actively support women in advancing their careers. * Goodbody inclusive recruitment and career development practices. * We support flexible work practices that enable colleagues to balance professional ambition with life outside work. **Benefits** * Competitive package with risk\-adjusted incentives. * Comprehensive benefits and professional development support, including access to leadership development programs and education sponsorship. **Employee Value Proposition** Goodbody is focused on helping our employees thrive, and we do this by bringing to life our Employee Value Proposition (EVP) ‘Together we prosper’. Our firm’s purpose is to create long\-term prosperity, and we cannot execute on our purpose without the right talent at Goodbody. To make sure we attract and retain industry\-leading, passionate and inspiring talent, we’ve built a vibrant culture based on inclusion, opportunities to grow and a commitment to ‘do the right thing’, one of our core values. We live this by offering: * Regular employee events through our universal inclusion programme fostering a sense of belonging and celebrating differences. And an ‘always on’ approach to social, financial, mental and physical wellbeing. * A benefits package that includes a generous pension scheme, educational sponsorship and health insurance, along with a hybrid working model to promote better work/life balance * Opportunities to give back through sustainability initiatives, volunteering and on\-site and online meetings with our charity partners Goodbody is an equal opportunities employer and is committed to fostering an inclusive workplace which values and benefits from the diversity of our workforce. Reasonable accommodations are offered at every stage of our recruitment process. Should you have a reasonable accommodation request please include a note in your application or email us directly at hr@goodbody.ie Please read the Goodbody Recruitment Privacy Notice. By submitting your personal data to us, you acknowledge that you have read and understood this Privacy Notice and agree to the use of your personal data in line with this. We will only use the information that we collect about you lawfully in accordance with the Data Protection Acts, 1988 and 2003 and the EU General Data Protection Regulation (GDPR). Appointment to this role is subject to the candidate's eligibility to work in Ireland. Where agency assistance is required, our Recruitment Team will engage directly with suppliers. Unsolicited CVs / profiles supplied to Goodbody by recruitment agencies will not be accepted for this role. Goodbody operates a direct sourcing model and where agency assistance is required, the HR team will engage directly with our recruitment partners. Goodbody Stockbrokers UC, trading as Goodbody, is regulated by the Central Bank of Ireland. In the UK, Goodbody is also subject to regulation by the Financial Conduct Authority. Goodbody is a member of Euronext Dublin and the London Stock Exchange. Goodbody is a member of the group of companies headed by AIB Group plc. For AIB Employees: Please note there are specific controls in place for transfers between AIB and Goodbody. Goodbody is part of AIB Group and you must inform your AIB line manager and HR BP in advance of making an application to Goodbody **Job Segment:** Investment Banking, Private Equity, Technical Support, Bank, Banking, Finance, Technology

Our way of working is called the Suir Way, designed to help us on a journey of continuous improvement. * Our people are empowered to make changes to ensure a quality install through effective ways of working. * Our people have a voice and feel empowered. * We have an in\-built culture of innovation, learning and continuous improvement. * Value is delivered every step of the journey. Our efficient processes deliver value. * We have a standard, consistent and fully integrated health, safety, environment, and quality system within the business. title: Podiatrist - Nursing Home/ Clinic Cover
description: **Who are we looking for?** For us at Spectrum it’s all about building the best team to help people achieve their health goals. This makes for happy patients and happy team members! We’re currently looking for a Full or Part Time Podiatrist to work collaboratively within a multidisciplinary team in our clinics and nursing homes in Dublin and Meath. The FTE salary range for this role is €50,000\-€77,000 per annum, with potential earnings of up to €100,000 with commission. As travel would be required as part of this role a drivers license and access to a car would be preferred. At Spectrum we want to offer you a career, not just a job. We provide clear career progression alongside a transparent salary scale to help our team develop clinically or into leadership roles. We look to cater for those who want to build their clinical skills and those who want to upskill to become a Clinical Lead, Manager or Director. Depending on experience, there is an option to deliver nail surgery! **Some reasons why our team joined us:** * Our comprehensive clinical support structure * Clear career pathways across clinical and leadership roles * Work variety – we provide multiple specialist services and provide training in each * Flexible working – we can cater for Full or Part time contracts and shifts to suit your lifestyle * CPD calendar including both internal and external speakers * CPD bursaries to assist funding external courses * Study leave * Robust systems to help our team provide the best care for their patients * Discounted Health Insurance * Annual leave that increases with length of service * Me Day – an additional day leave to try something new * Monthly Health \& Wellness allowance If this sounds like an opportunity you’re interested in, then take a read through the roles and responsibilities and hit apply below. We would love to hear from you! **What is the role and what are your responsibilities?** * Treat a variety of skin ailments such as verruca’s, corns, calluses, etc. * Assess and treat ingrown toenails, clipping toenails and general feet tidy up * Treat patients with diabetes at increased risk of foot disease who require preventative care and assistance * Determine the condition of a patient’s feet, ankles, or lower legs by reviewing his or her medical history, listening to the patient’s concerns, and performing a physical examination * Diagnose foot, ankle, and lower\-leg problems through physical exams, and other methods * Provide treatment for foot, ankle, and lower leg ailments, such as prescribing special shoe inserts (orthotics) to improve and correct foot and ankle deformities * Give advice and instruction on foot and ankle care and on general wellness techniques * Educate patients on how to improve and/or improve conditions * Liaise with other healthcare professionals, such as GP’s, Physios and other members of multi\-disciplinary teams, to exchange information about the background and progress of patients and Refer patients to other physicians or specialists if they detect larger health problems, such as diabetes * Meet continuous education requirements and maintain an up\-to\-date CPD folder * Write patient case notes and reports and collect statistics; as well as managing their payments, and issue receipts * Place stock orders and log repairs * In some cases, supervise student and junior podiatrists and enhance your teaching skills * Manage clinical risk and have excellent infection control awareness * Be caring, compassionate and professional and always have the patient at the core of everything you do * Help with creating blogs and content for social media and other marketing channels * Work with the marketing team and provide them with on the ground feedback from time to time **What experience and qualifications do I need?** * BSc OR MSc in Podiatry (essential) * CORU registration (essential) * Podiatry Ireland Membership (essential) * Professional Indemnity Insurance (essential) * Private practice experience (preferred) * New grads are welcome to apply **If you have any queries about the role, please contact 01 290 2690** Please note that if you click "apply" you consent to your information to be stored for 12 months for recruitment purposes by Spectrum Health.

title: Podiatrist - Out of Hours Weekdays and Weekends-Dublin
description: **Who are we looking for?** Spectrum Health is seeking an experienced and adaptable Contractor Podiatrist to join our growing panel of healthcare professionals. This position is ideal for an independent Podiatrist who values autonomy, clinical variety, and the ability to support patients in a face\-to\-face environment. If this sounds like an opportunity you’re interested in, then take a read through the roles and responsibilities and hit apply below. We would love to hear from you! **What is the role and what are your responsibilities?** * Treat a variety of skin ailments such as verruca’s, corns, calluses, etc. * Assess and treat ingrown toenails, clipping toenails and general feet tidy up * Treat patients with diabetes at increased risk of foot disease who require preventative care and assistance * Determine the condition of a patient’s feet, ankles, or lower legs by reviewing his or her medical history, listening to the patient’s concerns, and performing a physical examination * Diagnose foot, ankle, and lower\-leg problems through physical exams, and other methods * Provide treatment for foot, ankle, and lower leg ailments, such as prescribing special shoe inserts (orthotics) to improve and correct foot and ankle deformities * Give advice and instruction on foot and ankle care and on general wellness techniques * Educate patients on how to improve and/or improve conditions * Liaise with other healthcare professionals, such as GP’s, Physios and other members of multi\-disciplinary teams, to exchange information about the background and progress of patients and Refer patients to other physicians or specialists if they detect larger health problems, such as diabetes * Meet continuous education requirements and maintain an up\-to\-date CPD folder * Write patient case notes and reports and collect statistics; as well as managing their payments, and issue receipts * Place stock orders and log repairs * In some cases, supervise student and junior podiatrists and enhance your teaching skills * Manage clinical risk and have excellent infection control awareness * Be caring, compassionate and professional and always have the patient at the core of everything you do * Help with creating blogs and content for social media and other marketing channels * Work with the marketing team and provide them with on the ground feedback from time to time **What experience and** **q****ualifications do I need?** * BSc OR MSc in Podiatry (essential) * CORU registration (essential) * Podiatry Ireland Membership (essential) * Professional Indemnity Insurance (essential) * Private practice experience (preferred) * New grads are welcome to apply * Given the nature of this contract, applicants must be authorised to work in Ireland **What We Offer** * Flexible working hours that suit you in the evening during the week and/or weekends * Administrative support and efficient clinical systems * Opportunities to work within a multidisciplinary, patient\-centred healthcare team **If you have any queries about the role, please contact 01 290 2690** Please note that if you click "apply" you consent to your information to be stored for 12 months for recruitment purposes by Spectrum Health.

title: Process Engineer
description: Job Description: Tech Transfer Lead, Drug Substance Purpose The Tech Transfer Lead, Drug Substance , is responsible for leading the transfer of drug substance manufacturing processes from process development or sending unit into GMP manufacturing, including internal sites and external manufacturing partners. This role with the MS\&T team ensures robust technical transfer, process understanding, and operational readiness for clinical and commercial production of biologics. The position serves as a key cross\-functional interface among Process Development, Manufacturing, Quality, Analytical, Supply Chain, MSAT, Regulatory, and CDMOs to deliver successful, compliant, and timely transfers. Responsibilities * Lead end\-to\-end technology transfer activities for drug substance biologics processes, including cell culture, upstream and downstream operations, and related unit operations * Develop and execute transfer strategies, project plans, timelines, risk assessments, and readiness assessments * Partner with process development teams to transfer process knowledge, critical process parameters, control strategy, and material attributes into manufacturing * Coordinate creation, review, and approval of transfer documentation, including process descriptions, sample plans, bill of materials, protocols, reports, and technical summaries * Lead cross\-functional transfer teams and facilitate regular governance meetings to ensure alignment on scope, milestones, and risks * Support process scale\-up, characterisation, comparability, and process validation activities as required * Ensure analytical methods, sampling plans, raw materials, equipment, and facility requirements are aligned to support successful transfer * Identify technical and operational risks and drive mitigation plans to resolution * Partner with Quality and Regulatory functions to ensure compliance with cGMP, internal procedures, and applicable regulatory expectations * Support deviation investigations, change controls, and CAPA activities related to transfer execution * Provide technical oversight during engineering batches, PPQ, or process verification campaigns * Support training and knowledge transfer to manufacturing and quality teams at the receiving site * Monitor post\-transfer performance and lead continuous improvement initiatives to improve process robustness and transfer effectiveness * Manage communication with internal \& above site/sending unit stakeholders to ensure accountability and timely issue resolution Required Qualifications * Bachelor's degree in Biology, Biochemistry, Chemical Engineering, Biotechnology, or related scientific/engineering discipline * Typically 5\-8\+ years of experience in biologics process development, MSAT, manufacturing, or technology transfer * Strong understanding of drug substance manufacturing for biologics, including upstream and downstream processing * Knowledge of cGMP regulations, process validation, and quality systems in a regulated biotech environment * Demonstrated experience leading cross\-functional projects and technical teams * Excellent communication, organization, problem\-solving, and stakeholder management skills * Ability to travel as needed to support site\-to\-site or partner transfers Preferred Qualifications * Master's degree or PhD in a relevant discipline * Experience with monoclonal antibodies, recombinant proteins, or other biologic modalities * Experience with external manufacturing partners, CMOs, or CDMOs * Familiarity with process characterisation, scale\-down models, and comparability assessments * Experience supporting clinical and/or commercial tech transfers * Project management experience or certification

title: Product Owner (UX)
description: **About Revolut** People deserve more from their money. More visibility, more control, and more freedom. Since 2015, Revolut has been on a mission to deliver just that. Our powerhouse of products — including spending, saving, investing, exchanging, travelling, and more — help our 75\+ million customers get more from their money every day. As we continue our lightning\-fast growth,‌ 2 things are essential to our success: our people and our culture. In recognition of our outstanding employee experience, we've been certified as a Great Place to Work™. So far, we have 13,000\+ people working around the world, from our offices and remotely, to help us achieve our mission. And we're looking for more brilliant people. People who love building great products, redefining success, and turning the complexity of a chaotic world into the simplicity of a beautiful solution. **About The Role** The Product team shapes the experiences that power Revolut. They turn complex problems into simple, scalable solutions — collaborating across Design, Engineering, and Data to build features our customers love. We’re looking for a Product Owner who knows how to take responsibility over a product roadmap, including day\-to\-day operations, project execution, and addressing risks. From conception to launch and beyond, you’ll collaborate cross\-functionally and communicate with senior management to be the go\-to person for your feature. Up to shape what's next in finance? Let's get in touch. **What You’ll Be Doing** * Setting your team's goals, success metrics, and roadmap to align with company goals * Driving maximum impact based on data analysis, market research, and company strategy * Completely owning your team's products, from ideation, through development, to launch, growth, and maintenance * Working closely with Design and UX teams to define the customer journey and create an amazing user experience * Liaising with Engineering to ensure effective delivery of your product * Partnering with other stakeholders, such as Marketing, Legal, and Strategy teams **What You'll Need** * 3\+ years of experience at a high\-growth tech company or in banking/finance\-related business areas (acquiring, payments, investments, cryptocurrency, insurance, etc.) * 2\+ years of proven success leading consumer\-facing mobile products * 1\+ years of experience managing people * Outstanding communication skills * An eye for great UX/UI and impeccable understanding of design best practices * High analytical acumen along with solid business sense that guides ROI\-based prioritisation * Experience formulating and driving a focused product vision, and owning the narrative with leadership, customers, and partners * Experience leading product development and launch from scratch * A readiness to provide insight and deliver measurable results under ambiguous situations in a fast\-paced environment * An entrepreneurial drive to never settle **Nice to have** * A bachelor’s degree from a top university Building a global financial super app isn’t enough. Our Revoluters are a priority, and that’s why in 2021 we launched our inaugural D\&I Framework, designed to help us thrive and grow everyday. We're not just doing this because it's the right thing to do. We’re doing it because we know that seeking out diverse talent and creating an inclusive workplace is the way to create exceptional, innovative products and services for our customers. That’s why we encourage applications from people with diverse backgrounds and experiences to join this multicultural, hard\-working team. **Important notice for candidates:** Job scams are on the rise. Please keep these guidelines in mind when applying for any open roles. * Only apply through official Revolut channels. We don’t use any third\-party services or platforms for our recruitment. * Always double\-check the emails you receive. Make sure all communications are being done through official Revolut emails, with an @revolut.com domain. **We won't ask for payment or personal financial information during the hiring process.** If anyone does ask you for this, it’s a scam. Report it immediately. By submitting this application, I confirm that all the information given by me in this application for employment and any additional documents attached hereto are true to the best of my knowledge and that I have not wilfully suppressed any material fact. I confirm I have disclosed if applicable any previous employment with Revolut. I accept that if any of the information given by me in this application is in any way false or incorrect, my application may be rejected, any offer of employment may be withdrawn or my employment with Revolut may be terminated summarily or I may be dismissed. By submitting this application, I agree that my personal data will be processed in accordance with Revolut's Candidate Privacy Notice

title: Project Engineer
description: Life changing therapies. Global impact. Our strategy includes building teams across our global network to pioneer and shape the future of PCI. **Summary Of Role** Responsible for project engineering aspects within PCI Pharma Services including: * Specification, development, purchase and introduction of site facility, and production equipment tooling and processes in line with the companies and customers’ needs * Liaise with other departments and suppliers during the specification of new equipment to ensure fit for purpose is achieved. * Identification of future Capital investment projects to improve the business offerings and competitive edge * Raising of site capital application requests * To ensure that all engineering \& technical projects are introduced successfully into the business, on time and in budget. **Key Responsibilities** * Ensure safe work practices are being followed at all times, report any near misses immediately to the EHS Department. * Specification, development and purchase of site facility, equipment and tooling needs. * To ensure that all engineering \& technical projects are introduced successfully into the business, on time and in budget. * Coordination and execution of Factory acceptance (FAT) and Site acceptance (SAT) trials. * Ensure the setup of all technical documentation and paperwork such as tooling logs, setting sheets, on line working sheets, planned preventative maintenance schedules, schedules covering all new equipment and plant brought onto site on the technical services department. * Ensure all activities undertaken are in accordance with statutory, regulatory and company EHS, cGMP and technical standards, procedures and systems. * Ensuring that projects are achieved within the agreed timescale, cost and quality. * Maintenance of the technical operations and project engineering ways of working including but not limited to databases, specifications, templates, SOP’s and training. * Maintenance of the site tooling capability databases and specifications. * Maintenance of the Technical Drawing database. * Producing Technical Drawings \& Templates to support business \& customer needs this includes but is not limited to Tooling, Facility and Engineering drawings within scope of the job\-holder’s capabilities. * In conjunction with the operational group participate in Continuous Improvement Projects as required, including all OEE projects to ensure improved efficiencies, reduced waste and cost. * In conjunction the operational group assist in technical, product and process reviews to resolve operational, performance and functional issues. * Drive a Right First\-Time culture within operations to deliver a Zero\-Defect performance. * Deliver products of Value at Optimal Cost. * Monitoring future technologies and market trends identifying areas for potential investment including reporting and recommendations to senior and executive members of the PCI Pharma Services group. * Build, develop and sustain the knowledge, capability and competency with the Technical Operations team to support ongoing and changing technologies employed to meet the business objectives. * Create and sustain a working environment that promotes the PCI Pharma Services Values and Behaviours. * Connect across PCI Pharma Services to drive performance. * To undertake such tasks and to manage specific or ad hoc projects as and when required to meet department and business needs and within the scope of the job holder’s capabilities. * Carry out various other additional tasks that may be required from time to time in accordance with the overall goal of the company. **Essential** **Knowledge / Skills \& Experience** * Engineering degree (Level 7\)/ Trades qualifications/ Other suitable qualification * Two years’ experience working in an Engineering role. * Capability to set clear goals, communicate these clearly and ensure progress to completion meeting predefined targets. * Ability to manage multiple tasks and set priorities. * Problem Solving and Troubleshooting skills. The successful candidate should display a dynamic and proactive approach to manage problems. * Self\-motivated with a results driven approach. * Flexibility to work across different shifts on request in line with business needs. * Adaptable and ability to work collaboratively. * Proficient in Microsoft Office products (incl. Excel, PowerPoint, Word, etc.) * Previous office and operational based project engineering \& packaging experience in a Pharmaceutical or FMCG business * Detailed understanding of facility and engineering issues and processes. * A flexible and willing attitude * Communication skills to interact with internal and external customers at a senior level. * Strong Team player with open communication style with the ability to direct and train others and deliver clear instruction, including internal staff, suppliers, and tradesmen. * Design and develop new equipment to help improve line effectiveness. * Ability to influence and collaborate at all levels in order to achieve company targets. * Ability to represent the company in a professional and highly competent manner. * Ability to multitask and prioritise activities. * Ability to work on their own as well as part of a team. * Excellent problem\-solving skills. * Dependable, reliable and shows good integrity. * Ability to travel on company business as required. **Desirable** * Experience in a GMP or Regulated Environment. * Experience in Lean Manufacturing, including experience in Lean tools to develop continuous improvements. * Good working knowledge of cGMP, 21CFR Part11, and GAMP “Current”. * Detailed understanding of blister pack tooling design and manufacturing processes. * Use of creating/amending PLC programs.
